video.loadFailed: Could not load video — the format may not be supported.

preferences.enableAutoTrackingDescription: Shows the auto-tracking checkbox in the control panel for OpenCV-based automatic object tracking.
preferences.showVelocityDescription: When enabled, velocity components (vx, vy, speed) are available as choices on the kinematics graph axes.
preferences.showAccelerationDescription: When enabled, acceleration components (ax, ay, |a|) are available as choices on the kinematics graph axes.
preferences.enableMeasurementToolsDescription: Shows a measuring tape and angle tool panel for direct measurements on the video.

autoTracker.dragToSelect: Drag on video to select object to track
autoTracker.videoTrackingArea: Video tracking area — drag to select object to track

calibration.pointsTooClose: Points too close — move apart to calibrate

infoDialog.title: TrackLab Guide
infoDialog.loadVideoBody: Select a video from the dropdown — choose a sample clip, record with your webcam, or upload your own file.
infoDialog.coordinateSystemBody: Enable Axes in the control panel. Drag the origin to set the (0, 0) point, then rotate the axes to align with your scene.
infoDialog.calibrationBody: Enable Calibration in the control panel. Drag the two endpoints onto a known reference distance in the video, then enter the real-world length.
infoDialog.addTrackBody: In the Tracks panel, click “+ Add Track” to create a new track (A, B, C…). Up to 4 tracks can be active at a time.
infoDialog.digitizeBody: Select a track and click the object’s position in the video. Enable the Magnifier for precision. The video advances one frame after each click.
infoDialog.autoTrackBody: Enable Auto-Tracking in the control panel, then drag a selection box around the object. TrackLab will track it automatically across the remaining frames.
infoDialog.measuringTapeBody: Enable the Measuring Tape from the control panel. Drag the two endpoints to measure real-world distances anywhere on the video.
infoDialog.angleToolBody: Enable the Angle Tool from the control panel. Drag the three handles to measure angles in your scene. The vertex angle is shown in degrees.
infoDialog.kinematicsGraphBody: The configurable graph plots any two quantities against each other. Choose from position, velocity, acceleration, speed, or time on each axis.
infoDialog.dataTableBody: All digitized points appear in a table showing frame, time, and position. Click the CSV button to download your data.

a11y.digitizingArea: Video digitizing area — click to record the position of the active track
a11y.exportCSV: Export track data as CSV file
a11y.selectTrackForGraph: Select track to display on graph
a11y.graphPanelHeader: Graph panel header — drag or use arrow keys to move
